name: Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A malignant epithelial neoplasm that arises from the colon or rectum and invades through the muscularis mucosa into the submucosa.  The vast majority are adenocarcinomas. | Cancer that develops in the colon (the longest part of the large intestine) and/or the rectum (the last several inches of the large intestine before the anus).
parent_names: Digestive System Carcinoma|Malignant Colorectal Neoplasm

name: Childhood Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A colorectal carcinoma occurring during childhood. | A rare colorectal carcinoma that occurs during childhood.
parent_names: Childhood Carcinoma|Childhood Malignant Intestinal Neoplasm|Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Colon Carcinoma
definitions: A malignant epithelial neoplasm that arises from the colon and invades through the muscularis mucosa into the submucosa.  The vast majority are adenocarcinomas. | Cancer that forms in the tissues of the colon (the longest part of the large intestine). Most colon cancers are adenocarcinomas (cancers that begin in cells that make and release mucus and other fluids).
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Malignant Colon Neoplasm

name: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: The most common type of colorectal carcinoma.  It is characterized by the presence of malignant glandular epithelial cells invading through the muscularis mucosa into the submucosa.  Histologic variants include mucinous adenocarcinoma, signet ring cell carcinoma, medullary carcinoma, serrated adenocarcinoma, cribriform comedo-type adenocarcinoma, and micropapillary adenocarcinoma.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Digestive System Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Adenosquamous Carcinoma
definitions: An unusual colorectal carcinoma characterized by the presence of adenocarcinoma and squamous cell carcinoma components.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Digestive System Adenosquamous Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage
definitions: A term that refers to the staging of colorectal carcinoma according to the American Joint Committee on Cancer, 6th edition.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage
definitions: A term that refers to the staging of colorectal carcinoma according to the American Joint Committee on Cancer, 7th edition.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage
definitions: A term that refers to the staging of colorectal cancer according to the American Joint Committee on Cancer, 8th edition. This staging system applies to adenocarcinomas, high-grade neuroendocrine carcinomas, and squamous cell carcinomas of the colon and rectum. Appendiceal carcinomas, anal carcinomas, and well-differentiated neuroendocrine tumors (carcinoids) are not covered by this staging system. (from AJCC 8th Ed.)
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Carcinoma In Situ
definitions: An intraepithelial carcinoma arising from the colon or rectum without evidence of invasion.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Digestive System Carcinoma In Situ

name: Colorectal Mixed Adenoneuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that arises from the colon or rectum and is characterized by the presence of a malignant glandular epithelial component and a malignant neuroendocrine component.  At least 30 percent of either component should be present for the diagnosis to be made.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Digestive System Mixed Adenoneuroendocrine Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: An aggressive high-grade carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the colon or rectum. It is composed of malignant small or large cells. The mitotic count is more than 20 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20%.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Colorectal Neuroendocrine Neoplasm|Intestinal Neuroendocrine Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Sarcomatoid Carcinoma
definitions: A biphasic colorectal carcinoma with a spindle cell sarcomatoid component.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Sarcomatoid Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: A very rare colorectal carcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant squamous cell infiltrate.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Squamous Cell Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Undifferentiated Carcinoma
definitions: A usually aggressive carcinoma that arises from the colon or rectum and is composed of malignant cells which do not display evidence of glandular, squamous, or transitional cell differentiation.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Digestive System Undifferentiated Carcinoma

name: Early Stage Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A stage term referring to colorectal carcinoma that is limited to the colon/rectum or local lymph nodes, without metastasis to distant anatomic sites.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Hypermutated Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A colorectal carcinoma characterized by a high mutational rate caused by DNA mismatch repair deficiency or POLE/POLD1 driver mutations.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Localized Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: Colorectal carcinoma that is confined to the site in which it initially manifested without evidence of spread to other anatomic sites.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Localized Digestive System Carcinoma

name: Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that arises from the colon or rectum and has metastasized to another anatomic site.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Digestive System Carcinoma|Metastatic Malignant Colorectal Neoplasm

name: Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A colorectal carcinoma characterized by the absence of microsatellite instability.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Mismatch Repair Proficient Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A colorectal carcinoma in which the mismatch repair system is functional.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Rectal Carcinoma
definitions: A malignant epithelial neoplasm that arises from the rectum. The vast majority are adenocarcinomas. | Cancer that forms in the tissues of the rectum (the last several inches of the large intestine closest to the anus).
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Malignant Rectal Neoplasm

name: Rectosigmoid Carcinoma
definitions: A malignant epithelial neoplasm that arises from the rectosigmoid junction. The vast majority are adenocarcinomas.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Malignant Rectosigmoid Neoplasm

name: Recurrent Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: The reemergence of colorectal carcinoma after a period of remission.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Recurrent Digestive System Carcinoma

name: Refractory Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: Colorectal carcinoma that does not respond to treatment.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Refractory Digestive System Carcinoma

name: Resectable Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: Colorectal carcinoma that is amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Resectable Digestive System Carcinoma

name: Unresectable Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A colorectal carcinoma that is not amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma|Unresectable Digestive System Carcinoma

name: Advanced Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: Colorectal carcinoma that has spread extensively to other anatomic sites or is no longer responding to treatment.
parent_names: Advanced Digestive System Carcinoma|Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Childhood Rectal Carcinoma
definitions: A rare carcinoma of the rectum that occurs during childhood.
parent_names: Childhood Colorectal Carcinoma|Rectal Carcinoma

name: Colon Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma arising from the colon.  It is more frequently seen in populations with a Western type diet and in patients with a history of chronic inflammatory bowel disease.  Signs and symptoms include intestinal bleeding, anemia, and change in bowel habits.  According to the degree of cellular differentiation, colonic adenocarcinomas are divided into well, moderately, and poorly differentiated.  Histologic variants include mucinous adenocarcinoma, signet ring cell carcinoma, medullary carcinoma, serrated adenocarcinoma, cribriform comedo-type adenocarcinoma, and micropapillary adenocarcinoma.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Colon Adenosquamous Carcinoma
definitions: An unusual colon carcinoma characterized by the presence of glandular and squamous carcinomatous components.  The two carcinomatous components may be admixed within the tumor, or the two may appear separately in different areas.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Colorectal Adenosquamous Carcinoma

name: Colon Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage
definitions: A term that refers to the staging of colon carcinoma according to the American Joint Committee on Cancer, 6th edition.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage

name: Colon Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage
definitions: A term that refers to the staging of colon carcinoma according to the American Joint Committee on Cancer, 7th edition.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage

name: Colon Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage
definitions: A term that refers to the staging of colon cancer according to the American Joint Committee on Cancer, 8th edition. This staging system applies to adenocarcinomas, high-grade neuroendocrine carcinomas, and squamous cell carcinomas of the colon. (from AJCC 8th Ed.)
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage

name: Colon Carcinoma In Situ
definitions: An intraepithelial carcinoma arising from the colon without evidence of invasion.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Colorectal Carcinoma In Situ

name: Colon Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: An aggressive high-grade carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the colon. It is composed of malignant small or large cells. The mitotic count is more than 20 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20%.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Colon Neuroendocrine Neoplasm|Colorectal Neuroendocrine Carcinoma

name: Colon Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: A very rare colon carcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant squamous cell infiltrate.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Colorectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma

name: Colon Undifferentiated Carcinoma
definitions: A usually aggressive carcinoma that arises from the colon and is composed of malignant cells which do not display evidence of glandular, squamous, or transitional cell differentiation.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Colorectal Undifferentiated Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Adenoma-Like Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A well differentiated colorectal adenocarcinoma with good prognosis. It resembles a villous adenoma on the surface.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Villous Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Carcinoma Metastatic in the Liver
definitions: A carcinoma that originates from the colorectal area and has spread to the liver.
parent_names: Metastatic Carcinoma in the Liver|Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Carcinoma Metastatic in the Lung
definitions: A carcinoma that originates from the wall of the colon or rectum and has spread to the lungs.
parent_names: Metastatic Carcinoma in the Lung|Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Cribriform Comedo-Type Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare, invasive colorectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of malignant cribriform glands with central necrotic changes.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Cribriform Comedo-Type Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Goblet Cell Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An extremely rare colorectal adenocarcinoma that exhibits neuroendocrine differentiation and is associated with the presence of neoplastic signet-ring cells resembling goblet cells.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Goblet Cell Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Hepatoid Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare and aggressive extrahepatic carcinoma that morphologically and immunophenotypically resembles hepatocellular carcinoma and arises from the colon or rectum.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Hepatoid Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Large Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: An aggressive high-grade carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the colon or rectum and is composed of malignant large cells. The mitotic count is more than 20 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20%.
parent_names: Colorectal Neuroendocrine Carcinoma|Digestive System Large Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Medullary Carcinoma
definitions: A colorectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of sheets of malignant epithelial cells with vesicular nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. It usually has a favorable prognosis.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Large Cell Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Micropapillary Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare, invasive colorectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of clusters of malignant glandular cells within stromal spaces.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Micropapillary Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Moderately Differentiated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A colorectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant cellular infiltrate with 50-95% glandular formation.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Moderately Differentiated Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Mucinous Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An invasive colorectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of extracellular mucin pools that contain malignant glandular epithelial structures.  The extracellular mucin pools occupy more than 50% of the malignant lesion.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Mucinous Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Poorly Cohesive Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that arises from the colorectal mucosa and is characterized by the presence of isolated malignant cells or malignant cells that form small aggregates.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Poorly Differentiated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A colorectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant cellular infiltrate with less than 50% glandular formation.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Poorly Differentiated Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Serrated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare, invasive colorectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant infiltrate with serrated glandular architecture.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Small Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: An aggressive high-grade carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the colon or rectum and is composed of malignant small cells. The mitotic count is more than 20 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20%.
parent_names: Colorectal Neuroendocrine Carcinoma|Digestive System Small Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma

name: Colorectal Well Differentiated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A colorectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant cellular infiltrate with more than 95% glandular formation.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Well Differentiated Adenocarcinoma

name: Distal Colon Carcinoma
definitions: A colon carcinoma that arises from the splenic flexure, descending colon, or sigmoid colon.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma

name: Early Stage Colon Carcinoma
definitions: A stage term referring to colon carcinoma that is limited to the colon or local lymph nodes, without metastasis to distant anatomic sites.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Early Stage Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Hereditary Colon Carcinoma
definitions: Colon carcinoma that has developed in relatives of patients with a history of colon carcinoma.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Hereditary Malignant Neoplasm

name: Inflammatory Bowel Disease-Associated Colorectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A colorectal adenocarcinoma that develops in patients with a history of inflammatory bowel disease.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Localized Colon Carcinoma
definitions: Colon carcinoma that is confined to the site in which it initially manifested without evidence of spread to other anatomic sites.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Localized Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Localized Rectal Carcinoma
definitions: Rectal carcinoma that is confined to the site in which it initially manifested without evidence of spread to other anatomic sites.
parent_names: Localized Colorectal Carcinoma|Rectal Carcinoma

name: Locally Advanced Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A colorectal carcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to nearby tissues or lymph nodes.
parent_names: Locally Advanced Digestive System Carcinoma|Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Locally Recurrent Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: The reemergence of colorectal carcinoma after a period of remission, at or adjacent to the site of the original tumor.
parent_names: Locally Recurrent Carcinoma|Recurrent Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Metastatic Childhood Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: Metastatic colorectal carcinoma that occurs during childhood.
parent_names: Childhood Colorectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Metastatic Colon Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that arises from the colon and has metastasized to another anatomic site.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Metastatic Colorectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Colorectal adenocarcinoma that has spread from the original site of growth to other anatomic sites.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Metastatic Adenocarcinoma|Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Metastatic Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A microsatellite stable carcinoma that arises from the colon or rectum and has metastasized to other anatomic sites.
parent_names: Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma|Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Metastatic Mismatch Repair Proficient Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A mismatch repair proficient colorectal carcinoma that has metastasized to other anatomic sites.
parent_names: Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma|Mismatch Repair Proficient Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Metastatic Rectal Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that arises from the rectum and has metastasized to another anatomic site.
parent_names: Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma|Rectal Carcinoma

name: Metastatic Unresectable Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A colorectal carcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to another anatomic site and is not amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Unresectable Carcinoma|Unresectable Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Microsatellite Stable Colon Carcinoma
definitions: A colon carcinoma characterized by the absence of microsatellite instability.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Microsatellite Stable Rectal Carcinoma
definitions: A rectal carcinoma characterized by the absence of microsatellite instability.
parent_names: Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma|Rectal Carcinoma

name: Oligometastatic Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A colorectal carcinoma that has metastasized to a limited number of sites.
parent_names: Metastatic Colorectal Carcinoma|Oligometastatic Carcinoma

name: Proximal Colon Carcinoma
definitions: A colon carcinoma that arises from cecum, ascending colon, or hepatic flexure.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma

name: Rectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma arising from the rectum.  It is more frequently seen in populations with a Western type diet and in patients with a history of chronic inflammatory bowel disease.  Signs and symptoms include intestinal bleeding, anemia, and change in bowel habits.  According to the degree of cellular differentiation, rectal adenocarcinomas are divided into well, moderately, and poorly differentiated.  Histologic variants include mucinous adenocarcinoma, signet ring cell carcinoma, medullary carcinoma, serrated adenocarcinoma, cribriform comedo-type adenocarcinoma, and micropapillary adenocarcinoma.

parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage|Rectal Carcinoma

definitions: A term that refers to the staging of rectal cancer according to the American Joint Committee on Cancer, 8th edition. This staging system applies to adenocarcinomas, high-grade neuroendocrine carcinomas, and squamous cell carcinomas of the rectum. (from AJCC 8th Ed.)
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage|Rectal Carcinoma

definitions: An aggressive high-grade carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the rectum. It is composed of malignant small or large cells. The mitotic count is more than 20 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20%.

definitions: A biphasic rectal carcinoma with a spindle cell sarcomatoid component.

definitions: Rectal carcinoma that is resistant to treatment.

definitions: Colon carcinoma that is amenable to surgical resection.

name: Stage 0 Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6 and v7
definitions: Abnormal cells are found in the mucosa (innermost layer) of the colon and/or rectal wall. These abnormal cells may become cancer. | Stage 0 includes: Tis, N0, M0.  Tis: Carcinoma in situ: intraepithelial or invasion of the lamina propria.  N0: No regional lymph node metastasis.  M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 6th and 7th eds.)
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage|Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage

name: Stage 0 Colorectal Cancer AJCC v8
definitions: Stage 0 includes: Tis, N0, M0. Tis: Carcinoma in situ, intramucosal carcinoma (involvement of lamina propria with no extension through muscularis mucosae). N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th ed.)
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage

name: Stage I Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6 and v7
definitions: Cancer has spread from the mucosa (innermost layer) of the colon and/or rectal wall to the submucosa (layer of tissue under the mucosa) of the colon and/or rectal wall. Cancer may have spread to the muscle layer of the colon and/or rectal wall. | Stage I includes: (T1, N0, M0); (T2, N0, M0).  T1: Tumor invades submucosa.  T2: Tumor invades muscularis propria.  N0: No regional lymph node metastasis.  M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 6th and 7th eds.)
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage|Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage

name: Stage I Colorectal Cancer AJCC v8
definitions: Stage I includes: (T1, N0, M0); (T2, N0, M0). T1: Tumor invades the submucosa (through the muscularis mucosa but not into the muscularis propria). T2: Tumor invades the muscularis propria. N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th ed.)
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage

name: Stage II Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6
definitions: Stage Group II is subdivided into IIA and IIB on the basis of whether the primary tumor is T3 or T4, respectively.  T3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into the subserosa, or into non-peritonealized pericolic or perirectal tissues.  T4: Tumor directly invades other organs or structures, and/or perforates visceral peritoneum. (AJCC 6th ed.) | Stage II colorectal cancer is divided into stage IIA, stage IIB, and stage IIC. In stage IIA, cancer has spread through the muscle layer of the colon and/or rectal wall to the serosa (outermost layer) of the colon and/or rectal wall. In stage IIB, cancer has spread through the serosa of the colon and/or rectal wall but has not spread to nearby organs. In stage IIC, cancer has spread through the serosa of the colon and/or rectal wall to nearby organs.
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage

name: Stage II Colorectal Cancer AJCC v7
definitions: Stage II includes: IIA (T3, N0, M0); IIB (T4a, N0, M0); IIC (T4b, N0, M0). T3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues.  T4a: Tumor penetrates to the surface of the visceral peritoneum.  T4b: Tumor directly invades or is adherent to other organs or structures. N0: No regional lymph node metastasis.  M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 7th ed.)
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage

name: Stage II Colorectal Cancer AJCC v8
definitions: Stage II includes: IIA: T3, N0, M0; IIB: T4a, N0, M0; IIC: T4b, N0, M0. T3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues. T4a: Tumor invades through the visceral peritoneum (including gross perforation of the bowel through tumor and continuous invasion of tumor through areas of inflammation to the surface of the visceral peritoneum). T4b: Tumor directly invades or adheres to adjacent organs or structures. N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th ed.)
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage

name: Stage III Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6
definitions: Stage Group III is subdivided into IIIA (T1-2, N1, M0), IIIB (T3-4, N1, M0) or IIIC (any T, N2 M0).  T1: Tumor invades the submucosa.  T2: Tumor invades muscularis propria.  T3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into the subserosa or into non-peritonealized pericolic or perirectal tissues.  T4: Tumor directly invades other organs or structures, and/or perforates visceral peritoneum.  N1: Metastasis in 1 to 3 regional lymph nodes N2: Metastasis in 4 or more regional lymph nodes.  M0: No distant metastasis.  (AJCC 6th ed.) | Stage III colorectal cancer is divided into stage IIIA, stage IIIB, and stage IIIC. In stage IIIA, (1) cancer may have spread through the mucosa (innermost layer) of the colon and/or rectal wall to the submucosa (layer of tissue under the mucosa) and may have spread to the muscle layer of the colon and/or rectal wall. Cancer has spread to at least one but not more than 3 nearby lymph nodes, or cancer cells have formed in tissues near the lymph nodes; or (2) cancer has spread through the mucosa of the colon and/or rectal wall to the submucosa. Cancer has spread to at least 4 but not more than 6 nearby lymph nodes.In stage IIIB, (1) cancer has spread through the muscle layer of the colon and/or rectal wall to the serosa (outermost layer) of the colon and/or rectal wall or has spread through the serosa but not to nearby organs. Cancer has spread to at least one but not more than 3 nearby lymph nodes, or cancer cells have formed in tissues near the lymph nodes; or (2) cancer has spread to the muscle layer of the colon and/or rectal wall or to the serosa of the colon and/or rectal wall. Cancer has spread to at least 4 but not more than 6 nearby lymph nodes; or (3) cancer has spread through the mucosa of the colon and/or rectal wall to the submucosa and may have spread to the muscle layer of the colon and/or rectal wall. Cancer has spread to 7 or more nearby lymph nodes. In stage IIIC, (1) cancer has spread through the serosa of the colon and/or rectal wall but has not spread to nearby organs. Cancer has spread to at least 4 but not more than 6 nearby lymph nodes; or (2) cancer has spread through the muscle layer of the colon and/or rectal wall to the serosa of the colon and/or rectal wall or has spread through the serosa but has not spread to nearby organs. Cancer has spread to 7 or more nearby lymph nodes; or (3) cancer has spread through the serosa of the colon and/or rectal wall and has spread to nearby organs. Cancer has spread to one or more nearby lymph nodes, or cancer cells have formed in tissues near the lymph nodes.
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage

name: Stage III Colorectal Cancer AJCC v7
definitions: Stage III includes: IIIA (T1-T2, N1/N1c, M0); (T1, N2a, M0); IIIB (T3-T4a, N1/N1c, M0); (T2-T3, N2a, M0); (T1-T2, N2b, M0); IIIC (T4a, N2a, M0); (T3-T4a, N2b, M0); (T4b, N1-N2, M0).  T1: Tumor invades submucosa.  T2: Tumor invades muscularis propria.  T3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues.  T4a: Tumor penetrates to the surface of the visceral peritoneum.  T4b: Tumor directly invades or is adherent to other organs or structures. N0: No regional lymph node metastasis.  N1: Metastasis in 1-3 regional lymph nodes.  N1c: Tumor deposit(s) in the subserosa, mesentery, or nonperitonealized pericolic or perirectal tissues without regional lymph node metastasis. N2: Metastasis in four or more regional lymph nodes.  N2a: Metastasis in 4-6 regional lymph nodes.  N2b: Metastasis in seven or more regional lymph nodes.  M0: No distant metastasis.  (AJCC 7th ed.)
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage

name: Stage III Colorectal Cancer AJCC v8
definitions: Stage III includes: IIIA: (T1-T2, N1/N1c, M0); (T1, N2a, M0); IIIB: (T3-T4a, N1/N1c, M0); (T2-T3, N2a, M0); (T1-T2, N2b, M0); IIIC: (T4a, N2a, M0); (T3-T4a, N2b, M0); (T4b, N1-N2, M0). T1: Tumor invades the submucosa (through the muscularis mucosa but not into the muscularis propria). T2: Tumor invades the muscularis propria. T3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues. T4a: Tumor invades through the visceral peritoneum (including gross perforation of the bowel through tumor and continuous invasion of tumor through areas of inflammation to the surface of the visceral peritoneum). T4b: Tumor directly invades or adheres to adjacent organs or structures. N1: One to three regional lymph nodes are positive (tumor in lymph nodes measuring 0.2 mm or more), or any number of tumor deposits are present and all identifiable lymph nodes are negative. N1c: No regional lymph nodes are positive, but there are tumor deposits in the subserosa, mesentery, or nonperitonealized pericolic, or perirectal/mesorectal tissues. N2: Four or more regional lymph nodes are positive. N2a: Four to six regional lymph nodes are positive. N2b: Seven or more regional lymph nodes are positive. M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th ed.)
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage

name: Stage IV Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6
definitions: Stage IV colorectal cancer is divided into stage IVA and stage IVB. In stage IVA, cancer may have spread through the colon and/or rectal wall and may have spread to nearby organs or lymph nodes. Cancer has spread to one organ that is not near the colon and/or rectum, such as the liver, lung, or ovary, or to a distant lymph node. In stage IVB, cancer may have spread through the colon and/or rectal wall and may have spread to nearby organs or lymph nodes. Cancer has spread to more than one organ that is not near the colon and/or rectum or into the lining of the abdominal wall. | Stage IV includes: Any T, any N, M1.  M1: Distant metastasis. (AJCC 6th ed.)
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage

name: Stage IV Colorectal Cancer AJCC v7
definitions: Stage IV includes: IVA: (Any T, Any N, M1a); IVB: (Any T, Any N, M1b).  M1a: Metastasis confined to one organ or site (e.g., liver, lung, ovary, nonregional node).  M1b: Metastases in more than one organ/site or the peritoneum. (AJCC 7th ed.)
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage

name: Stage IV Colorectal Cancer AJCC v8
definitions: Stage IV includes: IVA: (Any T, Any N, M1a); IVB: (Any T, Any N, M1b); IVC: (Any T, Any N, M1c). M1a: Metastasis to one site or organ without peritoneal metastasis. M1b: Metastasis to two or more sites or organs without peritoneal metastasis. M1c: Metastasis to the peritoneal surface alone or with other site or organ metastases. (AJCC 8th ed.)
parent_names: Colorectal Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage

name: Unresectable Colon Carcinoma
definitions: A colon carcinoma that is not amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Colon Carcinoma|Unresectable Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Unresectable Colorectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A colorectal adenocarcinoma that is not amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Unresectable Colorectal Carcinoma|Unresectable Digestive System Adenocarcinoma

name: Unresectable Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A microsatellite stable colorectal carcinoma that is not amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma|Unresectable Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Unresectable Rectal Carcinoma
definitions: A rectal carcinoma that is not amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Rectal Carcinoma|Unresectable Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Adenocarcinoma in Colon Adenomatous Polyp
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that arises from an adenomatous polyp in the colon.
parent_names: Adenocarcinoma in Adenomatous Polyp|Colon Adenocarcinoma

name: Adenocarcinoma in Rectal Adenomatous Polyp
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that arises from an adenomatous polyp in the rectum.
parent_names: Adenocarcinoma in Adenomatous Polyp|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Advanced Colon Carcinoma
definitions: Colon carcinoma that has spread extensively to other anatomic sites or is no longer responding to treatment.
parent_names: Advanced Colorectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Colon Carcinoma

name: Advanced Colorectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Colorectal adenocarcinoma that has spread extensively to other anatomic sites or is no longer responding to treatment.
parent_names: Advanced Adenocarcinoma|Advanced Colorectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Advanced Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A microsatellite stable carcinoma that arises from the colon or rectum and has spread extensively to other anatomic sites or is no longer responding to treatment.
parent_names: Advanced Colorectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Advanced Rectal Carcinoma
definitions: Rectal carcinoma that has spread extensively to other anatomic sites or is no longer responding to treatment.
parent_names: Advanced Colorectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Rectal Carcinoma

name: Cecum Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma arising from the cecum.  It is more frequently seen in populations with a Western type diet and in patients with a history of chronic inflammatory bowel disease.  Histologic variants include mucinous adenocarcinoma, signet ring cell carcinoma, medullary carcinoma, serrated adenocarcinoma, cribriform comedo-type adenocarcinoma, and micropapillary adenocarcinoma.
parent_names: Cecum Carcinoma|Colon Adenocarcinoma

name: Cecum Carcinoma
definitions: A malignant epithelial neoplasm that arises from the cecum and invades through the muscularis mucosa into the submucosa.  The vast majority are adenocarcinomas.
parent_names: Malignant Cecum Neoplasm|Proximal Colon Carcinoma

name: Colon Carcinoma Metastatic in the Liver
definitions: A carcinoma that originates from the colonic wall and has spread to the liver.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma Metastatic in the Liver|Metastatic Colon Carcinoma

name: Colon Carcinoma Metastatic in the Lung
definitions: A carcinoma that originates from the colonic wall and has spread to the lungs.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma Metastatic in the Lung|Metastatic Colon Carcinoma

name: Colon Cribriform Comedo-Type Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare, invasive colon adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of malignant cribriform glands with central necrotic changes.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Colorectal Cribriform Comedo-Type Adenocarcinoma

name: Colon Hepatoid Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare and aggressive extrahepatic carcinoma that morphologically and immunophenotypically resembles hepatocellular carcinoma and arises from the colon.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Colorectal Hepatoid Adenocarcinoma

name: Colon Intramucosal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that arises from the colonic glandular epithelium and invades the lamina propria or muscularis mucosa but not the submucosa.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Intramucosal Adenocarcinoma

name: Colon Large Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: An aggressive high-grade carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the colon and is composed of malignant large cells. The mitotic count is more than 20 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20%.
parent_names: Colon Neuroendocrine Carcinoma|Colorectal Large Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma

name: Colon Medullary Carcinoma
definitions: A colon adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of sheets of malignant epithelial cells with vesicular nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. It usually has a favorable prognosis.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Colorectal Medullary Carcinoma

name: Colon Micropapillary Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare, invasive colon adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of clusters of malignant glandular cells within stromal spaces.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Colorectal Micropapillary Adenocarcinoma

name: Colon Moderately Differentiated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A colon adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant cellular infiltrate with 50-95% glandular formation.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Colorectal Moderately Differentiated Adenocarcinoma

name: Colon Mucinous Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An invasive adenocarcinoma of the colon characterized by the presence of pools of extracellular mucin.  Malignant glandular epithelial cells are present in the mucin collections.  Mucin constitutes more than 50% of the lesion.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Colorectal Mucinous Adenocarcinoma

name: Colon Poorly Differentiated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A colon adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant cellular infiltrate with less than 50% glandular formation.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Colorectal Poorly Differentiated Adenocarcinoma

name: Colon Serrated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare, invasive colon adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant infiltrate with serrated glandular architecture.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Colorectal Serrated Adenocarcinoma

name: Colon Signet Ring Cell Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An invasive adenocarcinoma of the colon characterized by the presence of malignant glandular epithelial cells which contain prominent intracytoplasmic mucin (signet ring cells).  The signet ring cells constitute more than 50% of the malignant cells.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Colorectal Signet Ring Cell Adenocarcinoma

name: Colon Small Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: An aggressive high-grade carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the colon and is composed of malignant small cells. The mitotic count is more than 20 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20%.
parent_names: Colon Neuroendocrine Carcinoma|Colorectal Small Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma

name: Colon Well Differentiated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A colon adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant cellular infiltrate with more than 95% glandular formation.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Colorectal Well Differentiated Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Adenocarcinoma Metastatic in the Liver
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that originates from the colorectal area and has spread to the liver.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma Metastatic in the Liver|Metastatic Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Low Grade Tubuloglandular Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An uncommon, low-grade adenocarcinoma that arises from the colon or rectum and is characterized by the presence of extremely well differentiated malignant small or medium sized glands with round or tubular shape and absence of desmoplastic stroma formation. It typically occurs in patients with long-standing inflammatory bowel disease and approximately 10% of inflammatory bowel disease-associated colorectal carcinomas are low-grade tubuloglandular adenocarcinomas.
parent_names: Inflammatory Bowel Disease-Associated Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Intestinal Low Grade Tubuloglandular Adenocarcinoma

name: Colorectal Signet Ring Cell Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An invasive colorectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of malignant glandular epithelial cells with prominent intracytoplasmic mucin resulting in the displacement of the nuclei.  The malignant glandular cells with intracytoplasmic mucin constitute more than 50% of the malignant cellular infiltrate.
parent_names: Colorectal Poorly Cohesive Adenocarcinoma|Signet Ring Cell Adenocarcinoma

name: Crohn Disease-Associated Colorectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A colorectal adenocarcinoma that develops in patients with a history of Crohn disease.
parent_names: Inflammatory Bowel Disease-Associated Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Localized Colon Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Colon adenocarcinoma that is confined to the site in which it initially manifested without evidence of spread to other anatomic sites.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Localized Colon Carcinoma

name: Localized Rectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Rectal adenocarcinoma that is confined to the site in which it initially manifested without evidence of spread to other anatomic sites.
parent_names: Localized Rectal Carcinoma|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Locally Advanced Colon Carcinoma
definitions: Colon carcinoma that has spread to nearby tissues or lymph nodes.
parent_names: Locally Advanced Colorectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Colon Carcinoma

name: Locally Advanced Colorectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Colorectal adenocarcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to nearby tissues or lymph nodes.
parent_names: Locally Advanced Adenocarcinoma|Locally Advanced Colorectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Locally Advanced Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A microsatellite stable colorectal carcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to nearby tissues or lymph nodes.
parent_names: Locally Advanced Colorectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Locally Advanced Rectal Carcinoma
definitions: Rectal carcinoma that has spread to nearby tissues or lymph nodes.
parent_names: Locally Advanced Colorectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Rectal Carcinoma

name: Locally Advanced Unresectable Colorectal Carcinoma
definitions: A colorectal carcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to nearby tissues or lymph nodes and is not amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Locally Advanced Colorectal Carcinoma|Locally Advanced Unresectable Digestive System Carcinoma|Metastatic Unresectable Colorectal Carcinoma

name: Locally Recurrent Rectal Carcinoma
definitions: The reemergence of rectal carcinoma after a period of remission, at or adjacent to the site of the original tumor.
parent_names: Locally Recurrent Colorectal Carcinoma|Recurrent Rectal Carcinoma

name: Metastatic Colon Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that arises from the colon and has metastasized to another anatomic site.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Metastatic Colon Carcinoma|Metastatic Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Metastatic Colorectal Mucinous Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Colorectal mucinous adenocarcinoma that has spread from the original site of growth to other anatomic sites.
parent_names: Colorectal Mucinous Adenocarcinoma|Metastatic Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Metastatic Microsatellite Stable Colon Carcinoma
definitions: A microsatellite stable colon carcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to another anatomic site.
parent_names: Metastatic Colon Carcinoma|Metastatic Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma|Microsatellite Stable Colon Carcinoma

name: Metastatic Rectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that arises from the rectum and has metastasized to another anatomic site.
parent_names: Metastatic Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Metastatic Rectal Carcinoma|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Metastatic Rectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: Rectal squamous cell carcinoma that has spread from the original site of growth to other anatomic sites.
parent_names: Metastatic Rectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Squamous Cell Carcinoma|Rectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma

name: Metastatic Resectable Colorectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Colorectal adenocarcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to another anatomic site and is amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Metastatic Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Resectable Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Rectal Carcinoma Metastatic in the Liver
definitions: A carcinoma that originates from the rectum and has spread to the liver.
parent_names: Colorectal Carcinoma Metastatic in the Liver|Metastatic Rectal Carcinoma

name: Rectal Cloacogenic Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that arises from the transitional zone at the junction of the rectum and anus.
parent_names: Rectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma

name: Rectal Cribriform Comedo-Type Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare, invasive rectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of malignant cribriform glands with central necrotic changes.
parent_names: Colorectal Cribriform Comedo-Type Adenocarcinoma|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Rectal Hepatoid Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare and aggressive extrahepatic carcinoma that morphologically and immunophenotypically resembles hepatocellular carcinoma and arises from the rectum.
parent_names: Colorectal Hepatoid Adenocarcinoma|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Rectal Large Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: An aggressive high-grade carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the rectum and is composed of malignant large cells. The mitotic count is more than 20 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20%.
parent_names: Colorectal Large Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma|Rectal Neuroendocrine Carcinoma

name: Rectal Medullary Carcinoma
definitions: A rectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of sheets of malignant epithelial cells with vesicular nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. It usually has a favorable prognosis.
parent_names: Colorectal Medullary Carcinoma|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Rectal Micropapillary Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare, invasive rectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of clusters of malignant glandular cells within stromal spaces.
parent_names: Colorectal Micropapillary Adenocarcinoma|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Rectal Moderately Differentiated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant cellular infiltrate with 50-95% glandular formation.
parent_names: Colorectal Moderately Differentiated Adenocarcinoma|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Rectal Mucinous Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An invasive adenocarcinoma of the rectum characterized by the presence of pools of extracellular mucin.  Malignant glandular epithelial cells are present in the mucin collections.  Mucin constitutes more than 50% of the lesion.
parent_names: Colorectal Mucinous Adenocarcinoma|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Rectal Poorly Differentiated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant cellular infiltrate with less than 50% glandular formation.
parent_names: Colorectal Poorly Differentiated Adenocarcinoma|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Rectal Serrated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare, invasive rectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant infiltrate with serrated glandular architecture.
parent_names: Colorectal Serrated Adenocarcinoma|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Rectal Signet Ring Cell Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An infiltrating adenocarcinoma arising from the rectum.  It is characterized by the presence of malignant glandular cells with prominent intracytoplasmic mucin.  These cells constitute more than 50% of the malignant cellular population.
parent_names: Colorectal Signet Ring Cell Adenocarcinoma|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Rectal Small Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: An aggressive high-grade carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the rectum and is composed of malignant small cells. The mitotic count is more than 20 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20%.
parent_names: Colorectal Small Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma|Rectal Neuroendocrine Carcinoma

name: Rectal Well Differentiated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rectal adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a malignant cellular infiltrate with more than 95% glandular formation.
parent_names: Colorectal Well Differentiated Adenocarcinoma|Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Rectosigmoid Mucinous Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An invasive adenocarcinoma of the rectosigmoid junction characterized by the presence of pools of extracellular mucin. Malignant glandular epithelial cells are present in the mucin collections. Mucin constitutes more than 50% of the lesion.
parent_names: Colorectal Mucinous Adenocarcinoma|Rectosigmoid Adenocarcinoma

name: Recurrent Colon Adenocarcinoma
definitions: The reemergence of colon adenocarcinoma after a period of remission.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Recurrent Colon Carcinoma|Recurrent Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Recurrent Rectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: The reemergence of rectal adenocarcinoma after a period of remission.
parent_names: Rectal Adenocarcinoma|Recurrent Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Recurrent Rectal Carcinoma

name: Refractory Colon Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Colon adenocarcinoma that is resistant to treatment.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Refractory Colon Carcinoma|Refractory Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Refractory Metastatic Colorectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma that is resistant to treatment.
parent_names: Metastatic Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Refractory Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Refractory Metastatic Digestive System Carcinoma

name: Refractory Rectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Rectal adenocarcinoma that is resistant to treatment.
parent_names: Rectal Adenocarcinoma|Refractory Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Refractory Rectal Carcinoma

name: Refractory Rectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: Rectal squamous cell carcinoma that is resistant to treatment.
parent_names: Rectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma|Refractory Rectal Carcinoma|Refractory Squamous Cell Carcinoma

name: Resectable Colon Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Colon adenocarcinoma that is amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Resectable Colon Carcinoma|Resectable Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Sigmoid Colon Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that arises from the sigmoid colon.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Sigmoid Colon Carcinoma

name: Sigmoid Colon Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that arises from the sigmoid colon.
parent_names: Distal Colon Carcinoma

name: Stage 0 Colon Cancer AJCC v6 and v7
definitions: Stage 0 includes: Tis, N0, M0.  Tis: Carcinoma in situ: intraepithelial or invasion of lamina propria.  N0: No regional lymph node metastasis.  M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 6th and 7th eds.)
parent_names: Colon Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage|Colon Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage|Stage 0 Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6 and v7

name: Stage 0 Colon Cancer AJCC v8
definitions: Stage 0 includes: Tis, N0, M0. Tis: Carcinoma in situ, intramucosal carcinoma (involvement of lamina propria with no extension through muscularis mucosae). N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th ed.)
parent_names: Colon Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage|Stage 0 Colorectal Cancer AJCC v8

name: Stage 0 Rectal Cancer AJCC v6 and v7
definitions: Stage 0 includes: Tis, N0, M0.  Tis: Carcinoma in situ: intraepithelial or invasion of the lamina propria.  N0: No regional lymph node metastasis.  M0: No distant metastasis.  (AJCC 6th and 7th eds.)
parent_names: Rectal Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage|Rectal Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage|Stage 0 Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6 and v7

name: Stage 0 Rectal Cancer AJCC v8
definitions: Stage 0 includes: Tis, N0, M0. Tis: Carcinoma in situ, intramucosal carcinoma (involvement of lamina propria with no extension through muscularis mucosae). N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th ed.)
parent_names: Rectal Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage|Stage 0 Colorectal Cancer AJCC v8

name: Stage 0 Rectosigmoid Cancer AJCC v6 and v7
definitions: Stage 0 includes: Tis, N0, M0.  Tis: Carcinoma in situ: intraepithelial or invasion of the lamina propria.  N0: No regional lymph node metastasis.  M0: No distant metastasis.  (AJCC 6th and 7th eds.)
parent_names: Rectosigmoid Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage|Rectosigmoid Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage|Stage 0 Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6 and v7

name: Stage I Colon Cancer AJCC v6 and v7
definitions: Stage I includes: (T1, N0, M0); (T2, N0, M0).  T1: Tumor invades submucosa.  T2: Tumor invades muscularis propria.  N0: No regional lymph node metastasis.  M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 6th and 7th eds.)
parent_names: Colon Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage|Colon Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage|Stage I Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6 and v7

name: Stage I Colon Cancer AJCC v8
definitions: Stage I includes: (T1, N0, M0); (T2, N0, M0). T1: Tumor invades the submucosa (through the muscularis mucosa but not into the muscularis propria). T2: Tumor invades the muscularis propria. N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th ed.)
parent_names: Colon Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage|Stage I Colorectal Cancer AJCC v8

name: Stage I Rectal Cancer AJCC v6 and v7
definitions: Stage I includes: (T1, N0, M0); (T2, N0, M0).  T1: Tumor invades submucosa.  T2: Tumor invades muscularis propria.  N0: No regional lymph node metastasis.  M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 6th and 7th eds.)
parent_names: Rectal Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage|Rectal Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage|Stage I Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6 and v7

name: Stage I Rectal Cancer AJCC v8
definitions: Stage I includes: (T1, N0, M0); (T2, N0, M0). T1: Tumor invades the submucosa (through the muscularis mucosa but not into the muscularis propria). T2: Tumor invades the muscularis propria. N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th ed.)
parent_names: Rectal Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage|Stage I Colorectal Cancer AJCC v8

name: Stage I Rectosigmoid Cancer AJCC v6 and v7
definitions: Stage I includes: (T1, N0, M0); (T2, N0, M0).  T1: Tumor invades submucosa.  T2: Tumor invades muscularis propria.  N0: No regional lymph node metastasis.  M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 6th and 7th eds.)
parent_names: Rectosigmoid Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage|Rectosigmoid Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage|Stage I Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6 and v7

name: Stage II Colon Cancer AJCC v6
definitions: Stage II includes: IIA (T3, N0, M0); IIB (T4, N0, M0).  T3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria or into non-peritonealized pericolic or perirectal tissues.  T4: Tumor directly invades other organs or structures, and/or perforates visceral peritoneum.  N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 6th ed.)
parent_names: Colon Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage|Stage II Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6

name: Stage II Colon Cancer AJCC v7
definitions: Stage II includes: IIA (T3, N0, M0); IIB (T4a, N0, M0); IIC (T4b, N0, M0). T3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues.  T4a: Tumor penetrates to the surface of the visceral peritoneum.  T4b: Tumor directly invades or is adherent to other organs or structures. N0: No regional lymph node metastasis.  M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 7th ed.)
parent_names: Colon Cancer by AJCC v7 Stage|Stage II Colorectal Cancer AJCC v7

name: Stage II Colon Cancer AJCC v8
definitions: Stage II includes: IIA: T3, N0, M0; IIB: T4a, N0, M0; IIC: T4b, N0, M0. T3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues. T4a: Tumor invades through the visceral peritoneum (including gross perforation of the bowel through tumor and continuous invasion of tumor through areas of inflammation to the surface of the visceral peritoneum). T4b: Tumor directly invades or adheres to adjacent organs or structures. N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th ed.)
parent_names: Colon Cancer by AJCC v8 Stage|Stage II Colorectal Cancer AJCC v8

name: Stage II Rectal Cancer AJCC v6
definitions: Stage II includes IIA (T3, N0, M0) and IIB (T4, N0, M0). T3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into the subserosa, or into nonperitonealized pericolic or perirectal tissues. T4: Tumor directly invades other organs or structures, and/or perforates the visceral peritoneum. N0: No regional lymph node metastasis.  M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 6th ed.)
parent_names: Rectal Cancer by AJCC v6 Stage|Stage II Colorectal Cancer AJCC v6

name: Stage II Rectal Cancer AJCC v7
definitions: Stage II includes: IIA (T3, N0, M0); IIB (T4a, N0, M0); IIC (T4b, N0, M0). T3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues. name: Submucosal Invasive Colon Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma of the colon that has invaded into the submucosa.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Invasive Carcinoma

name: Ulcerative Colitis-Associated Colorectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A colorectal adenocarcinoma that develops in patients with a history of ulcerative colitis.  Such carcinomas are usually multiple and flat.  Histologically, they are usually mucinous or signet-ring cell type.
parent_names: Inflammatory Bowel Disease-Associated Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Unresectable Colon Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A colon adenocarcinoma that is not amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Colon Adenocarcinoma|Unresectable Colon Carcinoma|Unresectable Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Unresectable Rectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rectal adenocarcinoma that is not amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Rectal Adenocarcinoma|Unresectable Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Unresectable Rectal Carcinoma

name: Unresectable Rectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: A rectal squamous cell carcinoma that is not amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Rectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma|Unresectable Rectal Carcinoma|Unresectable Squamous Cell Carcinoma

name: Adenoma of the Colon with Intramucosal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An intramucosal malignant glandular epithelial infiltrate in a colonic adenoma.  There is no evidence of submucosal invasion.
parent_names: Colon Intramucosal Adenocarcinoma

name: Advanced Colon Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Colon adenocarcinoma that has spread extensively to other anatomic sites or is no longer responding to treatment.
parent_names: Advanced Colon Carcinoma|Advanced Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Metastatic Colon Adenocarcinoma

name: Advanced Rectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Rectal adenocarcinoma that has spread extensively to other anatomic sites or is no longer responding to treatment.
parent_names: Advanced Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Advanced Rectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Advanced Rectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: A rectal squamous cell carcinoma that has spread extensively to other anatomic sites or is no longer responding to treatment.
parent_names: Advanced Rectal Carcinoma|Advanced Squamous Cell Carcinoma|Metastatic Rectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma

name: Cecum Carcinoma In Situ
definitions: An intraepithelial carcinoma arising from the cecum without evidence of invasion.
parent_names: Cecum Carcinoma|Colon Carcinoma In Situ

name: Locally Advanced Colon Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Colon adenocarcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to nearby tissues or lymph nodes.
parent_names: Locally Advanced Colon Carcinoma|Locally Advanced Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Metastatic Colon Adenocarcinoma

name: Locally Advanced Microsatellite Stable Colon Carcinoma
definitions: A microsatellite stable colon carcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to nearby tissues or lymph nodes.
parent_names: Locally Advanced Colon Carcinoma|Locally Advanced Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Microsatellite Stable Colon Carcinoma

name: Locally Advanced Rectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Rectal adenocarcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to nearby tissues or lymph nodes.
parent_names: Locally Advanced Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Locally Advanced Rectal Carcinoma|Metastatic Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Locally Advanced Rectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: Rectal squamous cell carcinoma that has spread to nearby tissues or lymph nodes.
parent_names: Locally Advanced Rectal Carcinoma|Locally Advanced Squamous Cell Carcinoma|Metastatic Rectal Squamous Cell Carcinoma

name: Locally Advanced Unresectable Colorectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A colorectal adenocarcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to nearby tissues or lymph nodes and is not amenable to surgical resection.
parent_names: Locally Advanced Colorectal Adenocarcinoma|Locally Advanced Unresectable Colorectal Carcinoma|Locally Advanced Unresectable Digestive System Adenocarcinoma|Unresectable Colorectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Locally Recurrent Rectal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: The reemergence of rectal adenocarcinoma after a period of remission, at or adjacent to the site of the original tumor.
parent_names: Locally Recurrent Rectal Carcinoma|Recurrent Rectal Adenocarcinoma

name: Rectosigmoid Signet Ring Cell Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An infiltrating adenocarcinoma that arises from the rectosigmoid junction and is characterized by the presence of malignant glandular cells with prominent intracytoplasmic mucin. These cells constitute more than 50% of the malignant cellular population.
parent_names: Colorectal Signet Ring Cell Adenocarcinoma|Rectosigmoid Adenocarcinoma
